key_insight: Thermal spray coatings can be engineered from the process level up to create micro- and nanoscale interfacial structures that improve ion/electron transport, reduce resistance, and extend electrochemical device lifetime.
engineering_relevance: A unified process-structure-property framework helps translate thermal spray parameters into durable, scalable coatings for batteries, fuel cells, electrolyzers, and solid oxide electrochemical systems.
method: Reviewed four thermal spray routes: atmospheric plasma spray, suspension plasma spray, high-velocity oxy-fuel, and cold spray.
Linked process parameters to porosity, phase distribution, inter-splat bonding, and interfacial chemistry.
Highlighted design strategies such as functionally graded interfaces, adaptive coatings, and machine learning-guided development.
evidence_signal: The paper is a review that synthesizes prior studies across multiple electrochemical systems to compare how coating microstructure and interfacial chemistry affect transport, resistance, and stability.
watch_item: Performance gains can be limited by porosity, non-equilibrium phase transformations, and other defects that are difficult to control consistently at scale.
paper_title: Thermal spray coatings in batteries, electrolyzers and solid oxide electrochemical systems
